  • Satellite connection not working on S25+

    I have a Samsung Galaxy S25 Plus and I have satellite connection on my plan. I've connected to it previously in a different area but recently I haven't been able to connect when I lose service. My phone just sits in emergency calls only. Is there something I should do to get it to connect again? I've looked thru settings but can't find anything. Thanks!!

  • Slow Internet even though speeds as high as 400mbps while it’s buffering

    My internet speeds are high but can’t even watch one tv connected to it without buffering and blurred picture. Have contacted t-mobile on multiple occasions and they fix it but it’s always short term. Sick of spending hours waiting on a person and going through each and every step over and over again. T-mobile has to be throttling my unlimited subscription. We can’t run multiple things at the same time as advertised. When streaming the tv show it won’t even load the guide so you can select another show.
    411702
    Visitor
    51 minutes ago
    19Views
    1like
    2Comments

  • Senior Citizens are second class

    We like T-Mobile’s widespread connectivity. It works like a charm in the EU. We were with them for many years and then we decided to go with another carrier that gave us a better deal. Then, eight years ago, we switched back to T-Mobile. Phone service has been great. We are on the Senior plan, but we have chosen the highest tier in that plan so it sure is NOT inexpensive. One of my patients told me that he recently went to Costco and was able to trade in his older phone for the latest model for a fraction of the cost. We both have older phones, so I was planning on not receiving that deal but close to it. I was quoted $800 dollars from one Costco location that I happened to be at and then I was going to my local Costco with the intent to upgrade but he quoted me only $200 or $300 because we are on the senior plan. At that point I thought, “how unfair to treat seniors differently from the rest of society”, so I decided to call T-Mobile customer service. I spoke with one person after having put in all the security information to connect and then they, in addition to that timely security clearance, asked for a pin number of which I was unaware. I demanded to speak to a supervisor, and I explained how unfair it was to be treated as a second-class customer just because I happen to be a senior. He agreed that it did, indeed, seem unfair and proceeded to offer me $1100 dollars per phone for trade-in and an additional $100 Costco gift card (which was part of the Costco offer). I told him that I would speak to my spouse to see which model phone we would want, and I was promised a call back on Saturday at 2:30 PM. I rushed home Saturday after work and waited, to no avail, for his phone call. I called yesterday, Sunday. I explained to the customer service representative that I would like to speak to a supervisor and that my last conversation should be on record as it was promised by the previous supervisor. She proceeded to tell me that there was no information about my call on the previous Monday except that I called and then promised to get a supervisor. After nearly a quarter of an hour, she also promised that I would be called back by a supervisor the same day. I never received a call back. Treating customers with such disrespect, and a senior at that, is uncalled for. Our society as compared to other areas of the world has been notoriously lacking in better treatment towards senior citizens. How unfair that a company like T-Mobile would perpetuate such behavior.
    RSpina
    Visitor
    1 hour ago
    4Views
    0likes
    0Comments

  • No router control

    The app to control the router is a joke. You cannot control the router at all. Where is bridge mode? I have the highest home internet and I literally have zero control of my router. I should start a new company that actually provides internet service instead of a paid way to steal your data.

  • Assurant nightmare

    On Tuesday 2/3 I made a claim on my Razr Ultra 2025. On Friday, FedEx gave me a box with a 2023 Razr inside. They have informed me that they couldn't send me the correct device until the other one was in the mail back to them. Got it in the mail today and now they can't see the tracking info. What do I need to do to get a working replacement for my device, not a model 2 years old?! Tmo care hasn't been helpful but is at least more polite than the Assurant people. Protection 360 is an overpriced scam.
    chazite
